key_insight: Dyna-Mat shows that foundation ML interatomic potentials should be judged in finite-temperature molecular dynamics, not only by static force errors, because structural and dynamical accuracy can still fail qualitatively for some systems.
engineering_relevance: For materials simulation workflows, the study helps identify MLIPs that offer the best accuracy-cost tradeoff for realistic molecular dynamics and highlights where pressure and ensemble properties remain unreliable.
method: Built Dyna-Mat-v1.0, a benchmark of condensed-phase first-principles MD trajectories at finite temperature.
Evaluated 15 foundation MLIPs across four model tiers using both single-point errors and MLIP-driven trajectory observables.
Compared structural, dynamical, and pressure predictions to construct an accuracy-cost Pareto frontier.
evidence_signal: The benchmark evaluates 15 foundation MLIPs on DFT MD trajectories, finding that lower force errors usually correlate with better ensemble observables, but pressure is poorly predicted across most models.
watch_item: Low force error does not guarantee correct finite-temperature structure, and pressure predictions may be limited by noisy or incomplete DFT stress labels in training data.
paper_title: Dyna-Mat: End-to-end benchmarking of foundation machine learning interatomic potentials in finite-temperature ensembles
